mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5% confirms labor market tightening and rising aggregate demand, which increases corporate profit forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below the 6.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and residential investment growth, which expands corporate revenue bases and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 105.0 indicates near-capacity utilization and supply-side constraints, which compresses short-term profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of periods where INDPRO exceeds 105.0.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the consumer price index above 2.5% signals accelerating inflation that erodes real equity returns and raises expectations for monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal shows no predictive power in a 2-year rolling t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year rolling windows, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate indicate synchronized supply-side expansion and labor market absorption, which boosts short-term market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.
